sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: calculate Q = worst case loss / geometric mean of losses, then K = account equity / Q, round down to integer contracts. Example: Q = $6,585.44 per contract.

source_rule_text: Trade 1 contract for every optimal f dollar amount calculated from the worst-case loss divided by the geometric mean drawdown, rounding down to the nearest integer for account size.
